frameworks.name: hierarchical generative model
frameworks.one_line: parameterized stochastic generator whose samples reproduce observed patterns

frameworks.name: variational lower bound
frameworks.one_line: tractable surrogate replacing intractable log-likelihood under exponential mode explosion

frameworks.name: self-supervised hierarchical learning
frameworks.one_line: unsupervised structure discovery via layered latent representations

frameworks.name: bottom-up / top-down cortical processing
frameworks.one_line: recognition and generative pathways mapped onto reciprocal cortical streams

core_question: How can one learn the parameters of a hierarchical stochastic generative model when each pattern admits exponentially many generative explanations?
key_assumptions: patterns arise from a parameterized stochastic generative model
each observation has exponentially many latent generation paths
direct maximization of observation probability is intractable
recognition and generation can be implemented by reciprocal pathways
manipulations: substitution of the intractable log-likelihood with an easily computed lower bound on observation probability
key_findings: an easily computed lower bound on observation probability sidesteps the combinatorial explosion of generative explanations
the resulting procedure operates as a form of hierarchical self-supervised learning
interpretations: the recognition/generation duality may relate to the function of bottom-up and top-down cortical processing pathways
